explicit_text: My great-great grandfather Louis Fiore was born in Agrigento, Sicily, and moved to Campania as a child before immigrating to New York City in 1898 with his family. He initially pursued his passion for art, designing and decorating furniture, but worked various jobs including brick mason and cabinet painter. Louis married Rose, another Sicilian immigrant, and later started his own furniture painting business during the Great Depression. He was a man who valued creativity and was not afraid to follow his artistic dreams despite economic hardships.

This place is the starting point of our family's journey before moving to mainland Italy and eventually to America.

explicit_text: Adele Fiore, my great-grandmother and daughter of Louis Fiore, was a trailblazer for women in the early 20th century. In 1920, she was attending New York University where she studied law, a remarkable achievement since NYU Law only began admitting women in 1890. Adele passed the bar exam before women even had the right to serve on juries, making her a feminist role model in my family and beyond.
